heading: Information on litigation management and cost and delay reduction
label: 479 Information on litigation management and cost and delay reduction
notes: References in TextThe date of the enactment of this chapter, referred to in subsec. (a), is the date of enactment of , which was approved .Pub. L. 101–650Dec. 1, 1990Sections 104 and 105 of the Civil Justice Reform Act of 1990 [], referred to in subsec. (c)(2), are set out as notes under .Pub. L. 101–650section 471 of this title

children.label: a
children.text: Within four years after the date of the enactment of this chapter, the Judicial Conference of the United States shall prepare a comprehensive report on all plans received pursuant to . The Director of the Federal Judicial Center and the Director of the Administrative Office of the United States Courts may make recommendations regarding such report to the Judicial Conference during the preparation of the report. The Judicial Conference shall transmit copies of the report to the United States district courts and to the Committees on the Judiciary of the Senate and the House of Representatives.section 472(d) of this title
study ways to improve litigation management and dispute resolution services in the district courts; and
make recommendations to the district courts on ways to improve such services.

The Judicial Conference of the United States shall prepare, periodically revise, and transmit to the United States district courts a Manual for Litigation Management and Cost and Delay Reduction. The Director of the Federal Judicial Center and the Director of the Administrative Office of the United States Courts may make recommendations regarding the preparation of and any subsequent revisions to the Manual.

The Manual shall be developed after careful evaluation of the plans implemented under , the demonstration program conducted under section 104 of the Civil Justice Reform Act of 1990, and the pilot program conducted under section 105 of the Civil Justice Reform Act of 1990.section 472 of this title

The Manual shall contain a description and analysis of the litigation management, cost and delay reduction principles and techniques, and alternative dispute resolution programs considered most effective by the Judicial Conference, the Director of the Federal Judicial Center, and the Director of the Administrative Office of the United States Courts.
